concepts.title: Combinatorial expansion
concepts.blurb: A $d$-regular graph $G$ on $n$ vertices is an $(n,d,\varepsilon)$-expander when every vertex set $S$ with $|S|\le n/2$ has $|N(S)|\ge(1+\varepsilon)|S|$ — small sets always touch many new neighbours, so the graph cannot be cleanly cut.

concepts.title: Spectral expansion
concepts.blurb: Equivalently, a $d$-regular graph expands when the second-largest eigenvalue $\lambda_2$ of the normalized adjacency $A/d$ is bounded away from $1$. Spectral expansion implies edge expansion (the easy direction of Cheeger), and the spectral gap $1-\lambda_2$ is the quantitative knob.

concepts.title: Cheeger inequality (graph version)
concepts.blurb: The graph Cheeger inequality $\tfrac{1}{2}\lambda_2 \le \phi(G) \le \sqrt{2\lambda_2}$ pins edge expansion between two functions of the spectral gap — the discrete shadow of Cheeger's Riemannian theorem and the bridge between combinatorial and spectral expansion.

concepts.title: Explicit constructions
concepts.blurb: Margulis–Gabber–Galil produced the first explicit expander family using $SL_2(\mathbb{Z})$ generators on $(\mathbb{Z}/n)^2$; Lubotzky–Phillips–Sarnak upgraded this to Ramanujan graphs of degree $p+1$ via Hecke eigenvalues; Reingold–Vadhan–Wigderson's zigzag product gives a combinatorial recursion that needs no number theory.

concepts.title: Applications
concepts.blurb: Expanders are the workhorse of pseudorandomness: error-correcting codes (Sipser–Spielman), derandomisation by walking on an expander, fast mixing of MCMC, and Reingold's $\mathrm{LOGSPACE}$ algorithm for undirected $s$–$t$ connectivity all reduce to a uniform spectral gap.

concepts.title: Ramanujan graphs and Alon–Boppana
concepts.blurb: Alon–Boppana shows any infinite family of $d$-regular graphs satisfies $\liminf \lambda_2 \ge 2\sqrt{d-1}$. A graph saturating this bound is called Ramanujan; the LPS construction realises Ramanujan graphs as Cayley graphs of $\mathrm{PGL}_2(\mathbb{F}_p)$, with the bound on $\lambda_2$ coming from Deligne's proof of the Ramanujan conjecture for weight-$2$ modular forms.
